steps.title: Turn the rate into table rows
steps.content: Mina runs at $6.4$ minutes per mile. That means $0$ miles takes $0$ minutes, $1$ mile takes $6.4$ minutes, and $2$ miles takes $12.8$ minutes.

steps.title: Find the line from the data
steps.content: The relationship is proportional because time equals rate times distance: $$\text{time}=6.4\cdot \text{distance}.$$ So the function is $$m(x)=6.4x.$$ The coefficient $6.4$ is the number of minutes per mile.

steps.title: Choose the matching function
steps.content: Choice D, $m \left(x\right)=6.4x$, matches the rate and the units: minutes per mile times miles equals minutes. The other choices use division, addition, or subtraction instead of multiplying by the rate. D is correct.
